metadata.label: conf/Whoop-SensorIntelligence
metadata.title: Sensor Intelligence Engineer II (Tools & Validation)
metadata.team_direction: WHOOP is on a mission to unlock human performance by empowering members through a deeper understanding of their bodies

findings.issue: Opening framing starts with a transition narrative instead of the target role, so the first read feels like a career-change note rather than a direct WHOOP-relevant engineering pitch.
findings.fix: Rewrite the first summary chunk to lead with the target identity and technical direction first, then place the data-analytics transition as supporting context.

findings.issue: The resume does not explicitly bridge marketplace/security/mobility work into WHOOP's sensor-intelligence and human-performance context, so the relevance is not immediately obvious to a first-pass hiring manager.
findings.fix: Add one bridge sentence that ties the candidate's validation tooling, ML evaluation, or data-quality work to sensor-like signal validation, time-series QA, or performance-oriented analytics.

findings.field: Experience > Software Engineer Intern | TikTok · Security > bullet 3
findings.issue: One bullet stacks TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, and scikit-learn together in a single claim, which reads like framework name-dropping rather than one concrete workflow.
findings.fix: Split the ML evaluation work into one primary workflow bullet and, if needed, a secondary support bullet so each framework has a clear role and the line reads as credible execution rather than stack dumping.

revision_priority: [MUST] Reframe the first Summary sentence to anchor on the target engineering identity first, and move the transition language behind that anchor.
[MUST] Split the TikTok ML-evaluation bullet so the frameworks are supported by a single workflow instead of being stacked in one line.
[SHOULD] Add one explicit bridge to WHOOP-relevant work, such as validation tooling, signal quality, or time-series/data QA, so the domain fit is legible on first scan.
[NICE] Tighten the top-of-page narrative so the resume reads as sensor/validation-adjacent rather than broadly cross-domain.
raw_json.revision_instructions: [MUST] Rewrite Professional Summary sentence 1 to lead with the target role/technical direction, then mention the analytics-to-engineering transition as background. [MUST] Rewrite the TikTok ML-evaluation bullet so one workflow owns the claim and the frameworks are not piled into a single line; keep evidence for the must-have ML stack, but distribute it across more than one bullet if needed. [SHOULD] Add one bridge clause to WHOOP-relevant work, such as signal quality, validation tooling, or time-series/data QA, in the Summary or TikTok project intro. Keep immutable company/title/date/location fields unchanged.
